topic: A person's online friend turns out to live in the same city
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a cheerful little banner proclaiming, "Hey! Guess where I am?" My jaw tightened so hard I thought my teeth might crack. I stared at the profile picture—a blurry shot of a downtown skyline I recognized instantly. My stomach churned, a tight, unpleasant knot forming just beneath my ribs. I scrolled through the recent posts, each one a fresh confirmation of the impossible. I reread the caption, the casual tone grating on my nerves. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of my cramped living room, my hands clenching and unclenching at my sides. This wasn't a coincidence; it felt like a cruel, elaborate joke played directly on my patience. I needed air. I needed distance. I threw my phone onto the sofa cushion with a sharp thud, the sound echoing the sudden, frantic energy surging through me.

[story 2]

I watched the map pin drop. It was right here. My city. The realization hit me like a physical blow, stealing the breath from my lungs. A hot flush crept up my neck, and I squeezed my eyes shut, fighting the urge to slam my laptop shut. Every shared memory, every inside joke we’d built over months of late-night chats, suddenly acquired a terrifying, tangible reality. My fingers hovered over the keyboard, trembling slightly. I began typing a reply, but the words wouldn't form. Instead, I just deleted, retyped, deleted again. The frustration built rapidly, manifesting as a shallow, rapid breathing pattern. I walked to the window, pressing my forehead against the cool glass, trying to physically push the image of their face—so close, so utterly real—out of my mind. It was infuriating how something so intangible online could become this heavy, suffocating presence in my actual space.

[story 3]

"Seriously?" I muttered under my breath, the sound rough and low. I reread the thread of messages, my eyes scanning for any clue I had missed, any subtle indication that this was a prank, a glitch in the matrix. Nothing. Just undeniable proof: my online confidant was geographically situated within a few blocks of my apartment. A strange, vibrating tension seized my shoulders. I started pacing again, faster this time, my footsteps sharp against the hardwood floor. My hands clenched into fists, the knuckles turning white. I wanted to shout, to demand an explanation, but the words caught in my throat, replaced by a hollow, simmering resentment. Why? Why would fate conspire to place this specific person in this specific location? The sheer improbability of it was overwhelming, curdling into a deep, unsettling irritation that demanded an immediate, forceful reaction.
